data.narrative:
Steady Energy is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Tommi Nyman, Hannes Haapalahti, and Petteri Tenhunen, currently valued at $110M post-money. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 9 companies—1 public (LNZA) and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage band, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis takes the $110M last post-money valuation and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A to later-stage advancement, implying $220M. Peer-median analysis references 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies with valuations ranging from $120M to $5.0B; the median across this cohort is $305M. The wide range reflects heterogeneity in the frontier-tech energy sector—Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B represents an outlier, while the tighter cluster around $300–$400M is more representative of mature Series B positions.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $187M (low) to $351M (high), with a median of $262M. This suggests current valuation at $110M sits materially below peer comparables, consistent with earlier-stage positioning. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiplier in a volatile frontier-tech sector, the relevance of the Commonwealth Fusion Systems outlier, and the extent to which Steady Energy's specific technology risk profile justifies discount or premium relative to the peer median.
